prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Mutant: Year Zero inspired post-apocalyptic RPG setting. Terrain type: continent_archipelago. Draw one dominant ruined-world continent plus a large irregular offshore archipelago in post-apocalyptic Zone cartography style. The mainland should include the Ark ruins encampment, dead zones of radiation, mutant clan territories, collapsed highways, scavenger trails, old-world ruin sites, survival zone sectors, toxic marshes, overgrown forests, cracked desert basins, bunker mountains, drowned city deltas, and rusted industrial belts. The archipelago should be uneven and varied: flooded skyscraper islands, broken bridge chains, quarantine isles, mutant settlements, green refuge islands, reef-like debris fields, old military platforms, and contaminated shoals. Avoid a simple vertical split; make the continent-and-archipelago separation a diagonal / C-shaped flooded gulf with twisting straits, bays, wreck channels, and broken coast shelves. Coastlines must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, reefs, and shoals. Large detailed survival atlas, grey-green toxic atmosphere, sector grid and hazard symbols allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
